programme.desc: The discovery of a Roman sword with traces of blood on it is linked to an unidentified, headless corpse found near Heathrow in March 2008. They are helped in their investigation by anthropologist Fiona Kennedy, for whom Dan begins to develop feelings - just as he is told his wife might be discharged from the secure hospital where she has spent the past several years. Tracy-Ann Obermann and Liz White guest star.(n)

Charlie Gimbert eventually buys the property, and agrees to rent the stables for Lovejoy Antiques. Patricia Hayes guest stars in an episode directed by Ian McShane.(n)

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: The dodgy dealer is threatened by a debt collector over a long outstanding sum and his hopes of meeting the repayment rest on a silver tankard, and hijacking a local auction administered by Charlotte Cavendish to obtain an antique piece of Japanese pottery. Neville feels the pressure as he is forced to home in on his target, and Oz faces a major setback in his relationship with Ofelia. Timothy Spall, Jimmy Nail, Tim Healy and Kevin Whately star.(n)

programme.title: Auf Wiedersehen, Pet
programme.desc: A terrified Neville is kidnapped, driven through the back streets of Havana and deposited at an empty house - where he is greeted by Pru from the Embassy, who asks him to trap rogue operative Tarquin. programme.desc: A serial arsonist targets sites related to the 1983 general election. Fantasy drama, with Dean Andrews and Joe Absolom (Doc Martin).(n)

programme.title: Ashes to Ashes

programme.title: Ross Kemp and Britain's Volunteer Army
programme.desc: Highlights of the recent series in which the actor celebrated the efforts of some of the UK's 750,000 volunteers by joining them as they went about their work. Among those featured are Debbie and Joanne from Surrey, who heard that staff at their local hospital, St Peter's, were struggling to get food when they finished their shifts, and decided to do something about it. Under a scheme called Nourish Our Nurses, they raised money to buy fresh food parcels, and with the help of an army of volunteers from Bishopsgate School in Egham they deliver 400 bags of fresh produce to the hospital every week.(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: The return of the award-winning documentary series following the work of the North West Ambulance Service as they care for the people of Liverpool and all across Merseyside. The shift begins with a call-out to a stabbing in a park - the 36th such incident the Merseyside teams have deal with in the past seven days. Then the medics prepare for a busy weekend as the St Patrick's Day celebrations hit the streets of Liverpool. Before long crewmates Paul and Emma are dispatched to a patient with chest pain, before treating another with a suspected broken arm. As the party gets into full swing, a call comes in from two children whose father has lost consciousness, and they have to be talked through the steps to revive him.(n)

programme.desc: Team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are joined by four more sporting stars for the quiz hosted by Sue Barker. The panellist are England cricketer Rory Burns, who made his international debut in November 2018, British athlete Emily Diamond, who was part of the bronze medal-winning 4x400m team at Rio 2016, former swimmer Mark Foster, who competed primarily in butterfly and freestyle at 50 metres and won a gold medal at the World Short Course Championships, and former rugby league star Robbie Hunter-Paul, who played for four English clubs during a 19-year career.(n)

programme.title: The Other One
programme.desc: Comedy about two sisters who share the same name, Catherine Walcott, but have no idea the other exists. This all changes when their father dies and it emerges he had kept the existence of long-term mistress Marilyn away from wife Tess. One sister, Cathy, has a fiance, a Duke of Edinburgh Award, and a pension. The other, Cat, has a pay-as-you-go-phone. The two siblings are complete opposites in every way, and the only thing they seem to have in common are their names. Both of them have always longed for a sister - just maybe not each other. Ellie White, Lauren Socha and Rebecca Front star.(n)

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: John has had a fancy new hands-free phone kit fitted in his car, but he arrives at Kayleigh's house only to discover she is far more excited by the previous night's events. Comedy about the daily journeys of supermarket employees John Redmond and Kayleigh Kitson, who have been thrown together in a company car share scheme, with each trip bringing fresh insight into their lives, as well as twists and turns in their unlikely relationship. Co-written by and starring Peter Kay and Sian Gibson, with Simon Greenall.(n)

programme.title: Our Coast
programme.desc: Adrian Chiles and Mehreen Baig explore Anglesey, the largest island in the Irish Sea. Mehreen recalls the story of the Menai Strait's magnificent bridges, while Adrian dons a wet suit to meet local paddleboarding legend Sian Sykes and see how she's playing a key role in helping rid the island of waste plastic. Plus, comedian Tudur Owen fulfils a boyhood dream by joining the Holyhead truckers at their brand new truck stop, Lucy Martin explores the shipwreck that changed the way weather is forecast and wildlife expert Patrick Ayree gets up close to the wild ponies of Newborough Warren.(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders of Britain
programme.desc: Neil Oliver investigates the hallowed sites of ancient Britain, looking at how people expressed their religious beliefs by reshaping the landscape around them. He begins by examining the very first stirrings of religion, visiting Nottinghamshire to view clues to a world of magic and ritual etched into the rock of Creswell Crags by Ice Age hunters, while tombs In the south of England and in the Scottish Borders are evidence of ancestor worship among the farmers of the Neolithic age.(n)

programme.title: Newsies: The Broadway Musical (2017)
programme.desc: A performance of the musical filmed at the Pantages Theatre in Hollywood, California. Set in New York City at the turn of the 20th century, the story follows Jack Kelly, a charismatic newsboy and leader of a ragged band of teenage 'newsies', who dream of a better life far from the hardship of the streets. Starring original Broadway cast members Jeremy Jordan, Kara Lindsay and Ben Fankhauser.(n)

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Cooking
programme.desc: The food writer demonstrates how spicy food works well with a cool accompaniment, preparing a chicken curry and tomato stew with yoghurt, as well as a new version of a traditional pudding. He also visits Bristol, where he meets a man who is passionate about pepper and combines it with a dish of soft fruit.(n)

programme.title: Big Dreams Small Spaces
programme.desc: In Blackpool, accountant Alex dreams of turning her boring backyard into a magical enchanted forest, complete with fairy lights and luscious woodland plants. Monty Don suggests a big and daring idea, and to illustrate his point, sends Alex to visit the ancient trees of Holker Hall in Cumbria. In Bristol, Ross has taken on a very overgrown allotment and wants a space to relax in as much as a vegetable garden. Monty boosts Ross' gardening know-how to help build his dream outside space.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: This edition revisits some of the stunning locations from this series, as the experts hunt out antiques and collectables at Greenwich Royal Naval College, Althorp House, Herstmonceux Castle and Reading Town Hall. Paul Martin visits Killerton in Devon and uncovers the fascinating story of Richard Acland who gave away his fortune for his political beliefs, and there are unexpected results in the saleroom.(n)

programme.title: Pointless

programme.desc: Keeper Tina tries to help a massive moose under attack from some pesky birds and Kate Humble is drafted in to help play cupid for a lovelorn emu.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Helen and Rob live in a Victorian terraced house in St Albans. They have put all their energy into renovating the entrance and the front room, but the back of the house remains a mishmash of tiny and unpractical rooms. Architects Robert Jamison and Laura Jane Clark compete to take control of this renovation project. Angela Scanlon presents.(n)

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Hedgehog champion Beryl Casey starts training a young apprentice to help her with the 30 hogs in her care. There is a trip down memory lane with Michaela Strachan as she celebrates Spineless Si the stickleback, and Iolo Williams looks at white-tailed eagles, as many more are now being sighted up and down the land.(n)

programme.title: QI

programme.title: The First Team
programme.desc: Mattie befriends star midfielder Carlos Velez in an attempt to improve team spirit. But hanging out with Carlos is not quite what he imagined. Jack braves a date with a girl he met online, Benji is approached by a mysterious businessman and Petey plays pranks on the squad. Football comedy from the creators of The Inbetweeners, starring Shaquille Ali-Yebuah, Jack McMullen, Jake Short and Theo Barklem-Biggs.(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Adventures
programme.desc: Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull explore their favourite inventions and scientific breakthroughs from the years between 1850 and 1900, beginning at Crossness Pumping Station in south-east London, which was part of the redevelopment of London's sewerage system. They then head to London Zoo, where they get their hands on a first edition of Charles Darwin's masterwork On the Origin of Species.(n)

programme.title: What's the Matter with Tony Slattery?
programme.desc: Tony Slattery was one of the most gifted TV comedians of the late 1980s and early 1990s, only to disappear from screens in 1996 amid rumours of a massive breakdown. As he approaches 60, he has been touring the country with a show that explores his past and his mental health. Although depression was diagnosed, he and his partner Mark Hutchinson are convinced he may have bipolar disorder, so Tony wants to have his mental health reassessed. But it will mean opening up about his addictions and a painful event in his childhood.(n)

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.sub-title: The Best of RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Monty Don and Joe Swift take a look back over the last decade of world-class design and horticulture from the RHS Chelsea Flower Show from their own gardens, beginning by celebrating the very best of British. From roses to royalty, they reveal more about the people and plants that fuel the nation's obsession with gardening, and meet the designers and the passionate people inspired by the UK's rich history and landscape.(n)

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: The next leg of Michael Portillo's journey begins in Skagway, Alaska, where he learns about writer Jack London, one of the gold prospectors who hiked to the Klondike 100 years ago. He then crosses into Canada on the White Pass and Yukon railway. Along the way he is invited to shake his tail feathers in a grouse dance, heads for the home of the Vancouver Giants, tries his hand at ice hockey and visits the Canadian Motion Picture Park.(n)

programme.title: Sacred Wonders of Britain
programme.desc: Neil Oliver visits Bronze and Iron Age sites that were important to ancient Britons. At Flag Fen near Peterborough he investigates a vast causeway that has sacred objects placed among its timbers, while at Maiden Castle in Dorset he finds evidence that human sacrifices were conducted to ward off evil spirits. On Anglesey, he learns about the rituals and dark reputation of the druids and how the Romans viewed them as dangerous religious extremists, and at Lullingstone Roman Villa in Kent he examines wall paintings of pagan water deities.(n)

programme.title: Letterbox
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Mel Giedroyc in which pairs of contestants go head-to-head to crack a series of passwords as they attempt to take home the £2,500 prize money.(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team goes island-hopping around the Western Isles and out to Shetland. Nick Crane hunts for gannets on Eriskay, and Neil Oliver hears the tragic story of a 1918 shipwreck off the Isle of Lewis in which more than 200 servicemen returning from the First World War were drowned. Plus, Miranda Krestovnikoff searches for otters which struggle to survive on the wild coast, and Tessa Dunlop investigates how the epic voyage of HMS Challenger in the 19th century first revealed the secrets of life in the depths of the world's oceans.(n)

programme.title: Astaire and Rogers Sing George and Ira Gershwin
programme.desc: A compilation of classic songs written by the brothers for musicals starring Fred Astaire and Ginger Rogers.(n)

programme.title: The Gay Divorcee (1934)
programme.desc: An unhappily married woman travels to an English seaside resort to escape, where she is pursued by a love-stricken young man. She mistakenly assumes that he has been hired to pose as her lover so her husband will divorce her, which leads to much confusion. Romantic musical comedy, starring Fred Astaire, Ginger Rogers, Edward Everett Horton and Betty Grable.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from the ancestral home of the Spencer family - Althorp in Northamptonshire - where antiques experts Will Axon and Charles Hanson search for treasures among the items brought in by visitors. Will uncovers some advertising memorabilia that has been dug out of the soil, and Charles is drawn to a 19th-century silver sewing kit. Paul also visits one of the largest show collections in the world to find out all about the weird and wonderful when it comes to footwear.(n)

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble and Ben Fogle catch up on the love stories at Longleat Safari Park. Today, there's huge excitement amongst the carnivore team as it seems the European wolves, captured mating previously, have given birth to pups. However, wolves are notoriously secretive so keeping an eye on these new arrivals is far from easy. Plus, love is in the air as two long-lost otters are reunited.(n)

programme.title: Your Home Made Perfect
programme.desc: Architects Laura Jane Clark and Robert Jamison compete to win a commission to remodel a home in Teesside for couple Ruth and Lindsey. The couple have lived in their 1990s developer home in Middlesbrough for years, and now they have two young daughters, the property fails to meet the needs of their family.(n)

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Iolo Williams and Gillian Burke are live from their home patches, joined by presenter and natural history cameraman Gordon Buchanan who looks back on his epic wildlife week in Scotland. The team reveals a unique story of habitat rewilding taking place on the Knepp Estate in West Sussex where white storks are breeding in the wild, while Chris is on a mission to explain why people should all love wasps.(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don adds a variety of herbs that thrive in shade to the herb garden and adds summer annuals to the cottage garden. Nick Bailey has taken a rare opportunity to design his town garden from scratch and gives tips on how to plan and lay out a path, while Carol Klein celebrates the hardy geranium and the abundance of varieties that give masses of colour to the summer garden. Plus, the programme finds a Wolverhampton couple who have created a garden full of mystery and imagination.(n)

programme.title: Later - with Jools Holland
programme.desc: With three platinum selling albums under her belt, Ellie Goulding chats online to Jools ahead of the release of her fourth this summer, which arrives 10 years after her debut record and first top 10 single Starry Eyed. The pair discuss the artists that have influenced her musical journey, illustrated by classic performances from the regular show's extensive archive. This edition also debuts Biig Piig, a musician who grew up between Ireland and Spain, and is now based in London. Recorded at home, she brings a taste of her sound with new single Switch, showcasing her unique brand of minimal pop.(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den: Best Ever Pitches
programme.desc: Joe Lycett narrates a compilation of some of the most memorable inventions from the show. The programme features a prototype for pedal-powered aviation, a device to stop couples from straying into each other's side of the bed, and the youngest ever entrepreneur to be backed by the Dragon.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Reporter Hilary Andersson travels from her home in Vermont to the dark heart of America's coronavirus crisis, New York City. Meeting doctors, health workers and community activists, she asks why so many people have died in a city with some of the best health care in the world. President Donald Trump insists his own handling of the pandemic saved thousands of lives despite initially hoping it would simply go away of its own accord, but the city's leaders face criticism too. Joining food queues in the Bronx, Hilary discovers how the city's poorest have suffered the most.(n)

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.sub-title: The Best of RHS Chelsea Flower Show

They are joined by Carol Klein, Adam Frost and the team to meet the international fleet of designers and plantspeople who travel thousands of miles to bring fresh ideas and fabulous floral creations to the show each year.(n)

programme.sub-title: Blue Peter: Disney Superstar and David Walliams
programme.desc: Long-running children's magazine programme.(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall explores habitats shared by humans and dangerous creatures, and helps remove a venomous snake from the garden of a family in South America.(n)

programme.title: Forces of Nature with Brian Cox
programme.desc: The physicist goes on a grand tour of the planet to explain how the Earth's beauty is created by just a handful of forces, beginning by examining the diversity of shapes in the natural world. Off the coast of Canada, Brian explains how massive icebergs that surge down from Greenland and into shipping lanes of the Atlantic emerge due to a powerful yet infinitesimal force of nature, while revealing how honeycombs made by bees in Nepal to store their precious honey conceal a mathematical rule.(n)

programme.title: Alexander and the Terrible, Horrible, No Good, Very Bad Day (2014)
programme.desc: An 11-year-old boy has a day where everything seems to go wrong, but gets no sympathy from his family. He becomes convinced that bad things only ever happen to him, but then his parents, his brother and his sister all suffer terrible days of their own. Family comedy based on Judith Viorst's series of children's books, starring Ed Oxenbould, Steve Carell and Jennifer Garner.(n)

programme.title: Back in Time for the Factory
programme.desc: Time moves on for the volunteers from 1973 to 1976, when disco was dominating the dance floors and maxi dresses were in vogue. It was also a time of great hope for women, with the Sex Discrimination Act having just been passed and the Equal Pay Act now in force. But will the changes in the law lead to equality on the factory floor or will the workers' hopes be dashed once again?(n)

programme.title: Nadiya's British Food Adventure
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain explores the culinary traditions of the Peak District and surrounding areas, beginning by putting her own twist on the classic Bakewell tart. She then joins Ian Barrett to learn how to make Derbyshire oatcakes, before using them as a casing for her potato and dill samosas.(n)

programme.title: The Great British Sewing Bee
programme.desc: The amateur sewer competition hosted by Joe Lycett continues. This time, the six remaining contestants take part in a series of challenges as they return to the sewing room to revive the glamorous, and sometimes ridiculous style, of the 1980s. Patrick Grant and Esme Young challenge the sewers to get down to business with the trickiest pattern so far, a woman's power jacket, before they are tasked with turning high-vis workman clothes into a party outfit in the Transformation Challenge. Finally in the Made to Measure Challenge, the contestants create their own versions of a cocktail dress.(n)

programme.title: The £100K House: The Final Fix
programme.desc: Architect Piers Taylor revisits people he and Kieran Long followed as they built their homes from scratch for less than £100,000, and helps them complete their projects. Sue and Tim still need an entrance hall staircase as a centrepiece to transform their home, but they require a series of low-cost lessons to have a chance of achieving high-end results. In north-east Scotland, Marcus and Sholto want to create outbuildings to stand alongside their rural home, and Piers presents them with a design that takes inspiration from a building on the banks of Loch Doine.(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A selection of film and television appearances made by actor and dancer Gene Kelly over the years. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.(n)

programme.title: Carousel (1956)
programme.desc: A criminal struggling to support his pregnant wife dies during a botched armed robbery, but is given the chance to return to life 15 years later to help his family and teach his daughter a valuable lesson. Adaptation of Rodgers and Hammerstein's musical, starring Gordon MacRae, Shirley Jones, Cameron Mitchell, Barbara Ruick and Gene Lockhart.(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: To get some tips for completing a complex fisherman's jumper, Ruth Goodman heads to pretty Polperro, and Tessa Dunlop takes the plunge into the glamorous history of British lidos.(n)

programme.title: Hugh's Wild West
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall discovers how the people and animals of Dartmoor live alongside each other, visiting an ancient hill farm housing dozens of house martins and swallows.(n)

programme.title: Cricket: England v West Indies Greatest Matches
programme.desc: A look back at the memorable 1984 Test from Lord's. An England side containing the likes of Ian Botham and Bob Willis took on a Windies team that cemented their reputation during the series as one of the greatest of all time.(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Private Pike receives his call-up papers and unexpectedly passes his medical with flying colours, much to the horror of his mollycoddling mother. Classic comedy first aired in 1975, starring Ian Lavender, Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier.(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's tour reaches its final stop, Singapore, where he learns about the island city's maritime origins and the vision of the 19th-century Englishman who put it on the map. At the bar of the Raffles Hotel, he treats himself to a Singapore Sling, now a world-famous cocktail, takes a boat down the river and dines with an artist and his family in Chinatown.(n)

programme.title: Eric Clapton: Life in 12 Bars
programme.desc: Documentary profiling the blues guitarist, narrated by the man himself, as he charts his six decades in the music business, and why his insatiable desire to grow his artistic voice led him to quit a stream of successful bands, from the Yardbirds and John Mayall and the Bluesbreakers to short-lived supergroups Cream and Blind Faith. Clapton also talks about his battles with drugs and alcohol, his love for George Harrison's wife Pattie Boyd and the tragic loss of his son in an accident. Including archive interviews with BB King, George Harrison and Jimi Hendrix.(n)

programme.title: Guitar Heroes at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive footage from BBC music shows of the 1970s. Including electric performances by the Rolling Stones, Eric Clapton, David Bowie, Motorhead, Nazareth, AC/DC and George Thorogood, as well as acoustic material by Michael Chapman, Horslips and Paco de Lucia.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints the scene from a cabin, hidden in the middle of a plush green forest and surrounded by the sounds of nature.(n)

programme.title: The Beauty of Diagrams
programme.desc: A look at the Pioneer Plaque, which was installed on the unmanned space probe Pioneer 10 to communicate facts about Earth and its inhabitants to potential life on other planets after its launch from Cape Canaveral in March 1972. The panel was engraved with graphic images and mathematical symbols, showing the location of its origin in the solar system and controversial naked figures of human beings.(n)

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Despite Miss Bingley's efforts to keep them apart, Darcy and Elizabeth's relationship grows more affectionate - until bad news arrives from Longbourn about Lydia and Wickham. Jane Austen adaptation, starring Jennifer Ehle, Colin Firth and Julia Sawalha.(n)

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, cameras in space show that the surface of the Earth is changing at a rapid pace as cities grow, forests disappear and glaciers melt. In ever growing grey of cities one man is feeding thousands of parakeets, while in Sumatra, a female orangutan and her daughter face life in a forest under threat. Elsewhere in Tanzania, local people use satellites to re-plant a forest, securing the future for a family of chimpanzees.(n)

programme.title: Chasing the Moon
programme.desc: A look at the Apollo 8 mission, when the crew became the first people to orbit the Moon and captured images that gave an entirely new perspective of the Earth.(n)

programme.title: Inside Einstein's Mind: The Enigma of Space and Time
programme.desc: David Tennant narrates a docudrama exploring the story behind Albert Einstein's general theory of relativity, which turned science's understanding of gravity, space and time on its head when it was published in November 1915. The film takes viewers on a visual journey that begins in Einstein's young mind, follows the thought experiments that gave him stunning insights about the physical world and ultimately reaches the extremes of modern physics.(n)

programme.title: The Art of Gothic: Britain's Midnight Hour
programme.sub-title: Blood for Sale: Gothic Goes Global
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon concludes his examination of England's Gothic Revival, focusing on the emergence of a new gothic fantasy horror following the outbreak of the First World War. He considers how gothic language came to make sense of the modern world - from Karl Marx's description of 'vampiric' capitalism, to author Joseph Conrad's dark vision of imperialism, and TS Eliot's critique of British society in his poem The Waste Land.(n)

programme.title: Fabric of Britain
programme.desc: An examination of the history of knitting, exploring how the craft rose in popularity during the 20th century with the help of the Beatles and 1980s breakfast television. The first programme considers the changing role of the hobby, including how it helped sustain some women through the hardships of war, and how knitwear items have become a staple of the British wardrobe. Narrated by Rebecca Front.(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: American artist Bob Ross offers further advice as he paints the scene from a cabin, hidden in the middle of a plush green forest and surrounded by the sounds of nature.(n)

programme.title: Earth from Space
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, cameras in space show that the surface of the Earth is changing at a rapid pace as cities grow, forests disappear and glaciers melt. In ever growing grey of cities one man is feeding thousands of parakeets, while in Sumatra, a female orangutan and her daughter face life in a forest under threat. Elsewhere in Tanzania, local people use satellites to re-plant a forest, securing the future for a family of chimpanzees.(n)

programme.title: John Williams at the BBC
programme.desc: Performances featuring the guitarist, from a BBC archive spanning 15 years. The programme includes classical masterworks, the prog rock of Sky and comedy with Eric Sykes. There are also duets with fellow guitar maestro Julian Bream.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Sybil Ruscoe and Jenny Powell present the edition first shown on September 28, featuring performances by Wet Wet Wet, the Beautiful South, Gloria Estefan, Sydney Youngblood, Kate Bush, Erasure, Black Box and Karyn White.(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: No Details Available.(n)

programme.title: Tom Jones at 80
programme.desc: A celebration of the singer's life and times, looking back over 60 years in showbusiness. This archive-based journey traverses Tom's career via some of his greatest songs and performances - from Delilah and It's Not Unusual to The Green, Green Grass of Home and Kiss. Additional context to Sir Tom's incredible longevity is provided through rarely seen footage and interviews with the man himself.(n)

programme.title: Tom Jones' 1950s: The Decade That Made Me
programme.desc: Tom Jones fronts the first of four retrospective documentaries in which celebrated musicians look back at the decades that defined them. For Tom, that decade is the 1950s, the era following the austerity of the Second World War that saw a boom in popular culture, which swept aside the old order and ushered in a new era of entertainment. The veteran singer provides a first-hand guide to his formative years in a small mining community in south Wales, revealing how he - much like many people of his generation - turned to TV, movies, radio and music to find a voice for himself. Revisiting Treforest and Pontypridd, where he spent his childhood and teenage years, Tom recalls his joy when rationing finally ended, his encounter with American GIs stationed near his home, and the arrival of rock 'n' roll and the 'Teddy Boys'. Includes contributions by writers Joan Bakewell, Katherine Whitehorn and Michele Hanson, and historians Alwyn Turner, Dr Martin Johnes, Tony Russell and Francis Beckett.(n)

programme.title: Tom Jones: A Little Later
programme.desc: The Welsh rocker's finest performances from the archives of Later with Jools Holland, including Baby It's Cold Outside, featuring Cerys Matthews.(n)

programme.title: Sounds of the Sixties
programme.desc: A selection of archive footage from the arrival of the so-called beat boom in 1964, which catered to the art-school craze for rhythm and blues. Featuring performances by Tom Jones, the Kinks, Manfred Mann, the Rolling Stones, John Lee Hooker and the Pretty Things.(n)

programme.title: Sings Elvis
programme.desc: Compilation of cover versions of Elvis Presley hits, originally shown in 2010 - the year that would have been his 75th birthday. Artists featured include Cliff Richard, John Cale, Paul McCartney and Tom Jones, and Carl Perkins and Mac Davis perform songs they wrote but which Elvis made famous.(n)

programme.title: Singer-Songwriters at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive performances from the 1960s and 70s, featuring Billy Joel, Joan Armatrading, Tom Paxton, John Denver, Bruce Johnston, Gordon Lightfoot, Chris de Burgh and Stealers Wheel. The programme also includes an unusual appearance by Ronnie Lane and Slim Chance on The Basil Brush Show in 1973.(n)

programme.title: Country Music by Ken Burns
programme.desc: The history of the American art form and how it evolved between 1953 and 1963 when the confluence of blues and hillbilly music at Sun Studios gave birth to rockabilly, the precursor of rock 'n' roll, with Elvis Presley and Johnny Cash at the forefront. In the recording studios of Music City, country music's twang was replaced by something smoother - the Nashville sound. Contributors include Loretta Lynn, Willie Nelson, Rosanne Cash, Elvis Costello, Brenda Lee and Merle Haggard. Narrated by Peter Coyote.(n)

programme.title: Tom Jones' 1950s: The Decade That Made Me
programme.desc: Tom Jones fronts the first of four retrospective documentaries in which celebrated musicians look back at the decades that defined them. For Tom, that decade is the 1950s, the era following the austerity of the Second World War that saw a boom in popular culture, which swept aside the old order and ushered in a new era of entertainment. The veteran singer provides a first-hand guide to his formative years in a small mining community in south Wales, revealing how he - much like many people of his generation - turned to TV, movies, radio and music to find a voice for himself. Revisiting Treforest and Pontypridd, where he spent his childhood and teenage years, Tom recalls his joy when rationing finally ended, his encounter with American GIs stationed near his home, and the arrival of rock 'n' roll and the 'Teddy Boys'. Includes contributions by writers Joan Bakewell, Katherine Whitehorn and Michele Hanson, and historians Alwyn Turner, Dr Martin Johnes, Tony Russell and Francis Beckett.(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In Argyll, George and Sybil set off to gather in their sheep from over six square miles of rugged mountainside before taking some of them to market, while soon-to-be-wed couple Martin and Mel introduce their newly purchased rams to their flock of ewes. In the far north beyond Inverness, John heads to market to teach his 13-year-old son James how to buy a good flock of ewes, and Sandy sets off on a 1,600-mile journey from his home on Lewis to sell his specialist Hebridean mutton and beef around the UK.(n)

programme.title: Nature's Weirdest Events
programme.desc: Chris Packham investigates unusual natural events, with the help of eyewitness accounts, scientists and footage of the occurrences.(n)

programme.title: Earth's Natural Wonders
programme.desc: The first of three programmes in which cameras explore some of the most inhospitable places on the planet and the people who live there, including a team of Sherpa roping a route on Mount Everest's notorious Khumbu Icefall and, on the slopes of Mount Kilimanjaro, farmers fight pitched battles to save their crops from marauding elephants. In the Amazon rainforest, two boys undergo a rite of passage involving hundreds of ants with the most painful stings on Earth, and conservationists in the Grand Canyon try to ensure the survival of one of America's few surviving condor chicks.(n)

programme.title: Billy Elliot (2000)
programme.desc: A northern youngster is pushed into taking boxing lessons by his macho dad - but secretly enrols in a ballet class instead, where it soon becomes clear he has a real talent for fancy footwork. However, he faces a tough time convincing his father that dance is a worthwhile pursuit. Comedy drama, starring Jamie Bell, Julie Walters and Gary Lewis.(n)

programme.title: The Bermuda Triangle: Beneath the Waves
programme.desc: Bruce Denardo explores the legend of the region where many planes and ships have disappeared in mysterious circumstances. Using a state-of-the-art submarine, Graham Hawkes reveals how five wrecks ended up 730ft down in the heart of the deadly expanse of water.(n)

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: Animated antics with the adventurous aardvark and his friends.(n)

programme.desc: Pieface steals Professor Von Screwtop's Pastry-based, Intelligent, Espionage unit (P-I-E) after forming a strong bond with the pie-shaped robot. Pieface is dismayed when Walter steals it from him to protect his Dad from the embarrassing footage the PIE's memory chip holds.(n)

programme.desc: Historical characters and events are explored in sketches and quizzes based on the books by Terry Deary. Comedy talent including Sarah Hadland, Steve Punt and Meera Syal help bring the strange facts, gory battles and crazy inventions to life.(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp and Britain's Volunteer Army
programme.desc: Highlights of the recent series in which the actor celebrated the efforts of some of the UK's 750,000 volunteers by joining them as they went about their work. Among those featured are Debbie and Joanne from Surrey, who heard that staff at their local hospital, St Peter's, were struggling to get food when they finished their shifts, and decided to do something about it. Under a scheme called Nourish Our Nurses, they raised money to buy fresh food parcels, and with the help of an army of volunteers from Bishopsgate School in Egham they deliver 400 bags of fresh produce to the hospital every week.(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: The return of the award-winning documentary series following the work of the North West Ambulance Service as they care for the people of Liverpool and all across Merseyside. The shift begins with a call-out to a stabbing in a park - the 36th such incident the Merseyside teams have deal with in the past seven days. Then the medics prepare for a busy weekend as the St Patrick's Day celebrations hit the streets of Liverpool. Before long crewmates Paul and Emma are dispatched to a patient with chest pain, before treating another with a suspected broken arm. As the party gets into full swing, a call comes in from two children whose father has lost consciousness, and they have to be talked through the steps to revive him.(n)

One sister, Cathy, has a fiance, a Duke of Edinburgh Award, and a pension. The other, Cat, has a pay-as-you-go-phone. The two siblings are complete opposites in every way, and the only thing they seem to have in common are their names. Both of them have always longed for a sister - just maybe not each other. programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich tackle a Salford woman's tired and uninspired garden. Nicole wants to make the most of the space and turn it into a place of nostalgia by re-creating the garden of her childhood in Germany.(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp and Britain's Volunteer Army
programme.desc: Highlights of the recent series in which the actor celebrated the efforts of some of the UK's 750,000 volunteers by joining them as they went about their work. Among those featured are Debbie and Joanne from Surrey, who heard that staff at their local hospital, St Peter's, were struggling to get food when they finished their shifts, and decided to do something about it. Under a scheme called Nourish Our Nurses, they raised money to buy fresh food parcels, and with the help of an army of volunteers from Bishopsgate School in Egham they deliver 400 bags of fresh produce to the hospital every week.(n)

programme.title: Ambulance - Liverpool
programme.desc: The return of the award-winning documentary series following the work of the North West Ambulance Service as they care for the people of Liverpool and all across Merseyside. The shift begins with a call-out to a stabbing in a park - the 36th such incident the Merseyside teams have deal with in the past seven days. Then the medics prepare for a busy weekend as the St Patrick's Day celebrations hit the streets of Liverpool. Before long crewmates Paul and Emma are dispatched to a patient with chest pain, before treating another with a suspected broken arm. As the party gets into full swing, a call comes in from two children whose father has lost consciousness, and they have to be talked through the steps to revive him.(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson takes the reins of a rural property search in Gloucestershire with a horse-loving husband and wife who have a £1.2million budget and a desire to upscale their country lifestyle. programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Anu and Ash live in Coventry with their cat Quinn, where their garden is currently a new build blank canvas divided into two cramped tiers. The couple want to turn it into a pet-friendly delight, inspired by the gardens and countryside they fell in love with on holidays in Tuscany, Italy. They want lots of interest for Quinn and a large inviting patio where they can entertain their friends and family. Arit Anderson and Charlie Dimmock have a £3,500 budget to make the couple's dreams come true.(n)

programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson.(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones hosts the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances.(n)

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond and Nick Nairn are in their own kitchens cooking with Scottish produce and Euan McIlwraith finds out how you can research the history of your own area using historic maps. Anne Lundon investigates how lockdown has impacted the National Trust for Scotland and the important work of the Air Ambulance.(n)

programme.title: My Family
programme.desc: The Harpers' thoughts turn to death. Michael worries about life expectancy and Ben wonders why God has not made the process of dying as enjoyable as sex. Meanwhile, Susan is shaken by bad news about a lost tourist.(n)

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: When Charlie's school gets a bad Ofsted rating, Lee and Lucy start looking around for alternative schools. Despite Frank's protests, Geoffrey and Wendy offer to pay to send Charlie to be privately educated, which presents Lee and Lucy with a moral dilemma.(n)

programme.title: The Other One
programme.desc: Comedy about two sisters who share the same name, Catherine Walcott, but have no idea the other exists. This all changes when their father dies and it emerges he had kept the existence of long-term mistress Marilyn away from wife Tess. One sister, Cathy, has a fiance, a Duke of Edinburgh Award, and a pension. The other, Cat, has a pay-as-you-go-phone. The two siblings are complete opposites in every way, and the only thing they seem to have in common are their names. Both of them have always longed for a sister - just maybe not each other. Ellie White, Lauren Socha and Rebecca Front star.(n)

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: John has had a fancy new hands-free phone kit fitted in his car, but he arrives at Kayleigh's house only to discover she is far more excited by the previous night's events. Comedy about the daily journeys of supermarket employees John Redmond and Kayleigh Kitson, who have been thrown together in a company car share scheme, with each trip bringing fresh insight into their lives, as well as twists and turns in their unlikely relationship. Co-written by and starring Peter Kay and Sian Gibson, with Simon Greenall.(n)

programme.desc: Women are given make-unders so they have a more natural look before being sent on a series of dates with specially selected suitors.(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell are joined by four more sporting stars for the quiz hosted by Sue Barker. The panellist are England cricketer Rory Burns, who made his international debut in November 2018, British athlete Emily Diamond, who was part of the bronze medal-winning 4x400m team at Rio 2016, former swimmer Mark Foster, who competed primarily in butterfly and freestyle at 50 metres and won a gold medal at the World Short Course Championships, and former rugby league star Robbie Hunter-Paul, who played for four English clubs during a 19-year career.(n)

programme.title: I Give It a Year (2013)
programme.desc: A couple marry after a whirlwind romance, but family and friends have doubts about the relationship. As their first anniversary approaches, they both begin to wonder if they are right for each other, especially when faced with more appealing alternative partners. Romantic comedy, with Rose Byrne, Rafe Spall, Anna Faris and Simon Baker.(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Laughs
programme.desc: The comedian looks back at some of the best moments from the Michael McIntyre's Big Show series, revisiting some of the biggest laughs from the past five seasons. The host also relives the moment Gary Barlow surprises the audience by singing along to their karaoke versions of some classic Take That songs.(n)

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Laughs
programme.desc: The comedian looks back at some of the best moments from the Michael McIntyre's Big Show series, revisiting some of the biggest laughs from the past five seasons. Tonight, he looks back on the shocked look on Judge Rinder's face as he is woken from his sleep to play the Midnight Gameshow, and the unforgettable Send to Alls with Holly Willoughby and Jamie Oliver. The host also relives the moment Gary Barlow surprises the audience by singing along to their karaoke versions of some classic Take That songs.(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Reporter Hilary Andersson travels from her home in Vermont to the dark heart of America's coronavirus crisis, New York City. Meeting doctors, health workers and community activists, she asks why so many people have died in a city with some of the best health care in the world. President Donald Trump insists his own handling of the pandemic saved thousands of lives despite initially hoping it would simply go away of its own accord, but the city's leaders face criticism too. Joining food queues in the Bronx, Hilary discovers how the city's poorest have suffered the most.(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.sub-title: Car Crime: Is My Car Safe? Tonight
programme.desc: Government figures reveal car crime has risen for the past six years. It has even continued in lockdown in some areas. As more motorists are targeted by organised gangs, Alex Beresford investigates what people can do to steer clear of vehicle crime.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family
programme.desc: John Barnes and Matt Le Tissier were both flying the flag for England in the recent ITV show Harry's Heroes - but now they're on different sides as they take part in the knockout quiz, captaining teams comprising members of their own families in a bid to make it through to the next round. At stake for the eventual winners is £25,000 for their chosen charity and the coveted title of Britain's Brightest Celebrity Family. The Chase star Anne 'The Governess' Hegerty is the one asking the questions for once.(n)

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show: Live
programme.desc: As life in lockdown continues, the financial journalist presents more advice for people who have found themselves living with financial uncertainty. Martin answers viewers' questions on how the economic crisis affects family finances, jobs and consumer rights.(n)

programme.title: Unbelievable Moments Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong presents a selection of extraordinary eyewitness footage filmed by members of the public, including the heart-stopping story of a man who took a ‘pleasure flight' on a hang-glider. Two passengers on board the Viking Sky cruise ship that suffered engine failure in rough seas just off the coast of Norway recount how they were left fearing for their lives, while a heroic cop throws the rulebook out of the window when trying to save a baby's life on the roadside.(n)

programme.sub-title: Tonight I'll Be Staying Here with You
programme.desc: The lifts in Auntie Vera's block of flats are playing up, which is no good with her hips, so she announces to Sharon and Tracey that she is moving in with them. There's just one little snag - the only available room is Dorien's - and she is somewhat dismayed to be told that family comes first. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph, with a guest appearance from Kate Williams (Love Thy Neighbour).(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates the death of a pensioner found stabbed on a train at the height of rush hour. The case has a huge impact on Joe's home life when his daughter is named as a key witness, and he struggles to maintain a professional distance. The trail leads the team to a quiet Northumberland coastal town where clues appear to lead to one particular street - but why are the residents so reluctant to talk? Starring Brenda Blethyn, with David Leon, Sonya Cassidy, Jon Morrison and Eva Birthistle.(n)

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: A chance to catch the original US version of the show in which mystery celebrities battle it out in a singing contest while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers dressed as Butterfly, Egg, Thingamajig and Skeleton take to the stage, while panellists Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Nicole Scherzinger, and regular star of the British spin-off Ken Jeong try to work out who is behind the mask.(n)

programme.title: Ninja Warrior UK
programme.desc: Competitors from across the nation test their strength, speed and courage in this obstacle-course challenge, hosted by Ben Shephard, Rochelle Humes and Chris Kamara. Those who make it to the end of the adrenaline-fuelled mission face one final task - scaling the heights of a tower named Mount Midoriyama - as they battle it out for the title of Ninja Warrior UK.(n)

programme.title: Alan Carr's Epic Gameshow
programme.desc: The comedian revives another classic game show with an episode of The Price Is Right. programme.title: Casino Royale (2006)
programme.desc: James Bond achieves his '00' status and is dispatched on the trail of Le Chiffre, an international criminal financing terrorist organisations around the world. Bond's investigation leads to a casino in Montenegro, where he takes part in a high-stakes poker game in a bid to bankrupt Le Chiffre and put an end to his plans. Spy thriller, directed by Martin Campbell and starring Daniel Craig in his first outing as 007, with Eva Green, Mads Mikkelsen, Judi Dench and Giancarlo Giannini.(n)

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and his team come to the aid of nonagenarian Roberta and her 86-year-old best friend and carer Enid, who live together in a retirement bungalow in Wiveliscombe, Somerset. Originally from Liverpool, the two plucky pensioners have helped each other out countless times throughout the course of their friendship, which has lasted seven decades. However, they now find themselves trapped in their home by a treacherous slope in their garden, so Alan plans to set them free by designing a future-proofed garden in a vintage style.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff gains entry to Number 6 to delete the CCTV footage, Roy finds Shona lurking outside the café, and Dev decides to move the twins to Oakhill School.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alya makes a shocking discovery regarding Geoff, Asha's problems follow her to her new school and Roy invites Shona to move into his flat.(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Geoff is forced to come clean about hiring escorts, Asha finds a renewed confidence and Shona agrees to live with Roy rather than David.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: An actress engaged to marry a foreign prince becomes the police's number one suspect in the murder of a shady producer. Desperate to ensure she escapes prosecution, her wealthy fiance asks Poirot to catch the real killer. Unfortunately, the celebrated detective ends up concluding she has lied - or is an accomplice. David Suchet stars, with Niamh Cusack, Abigail Cruttenden and Sean Pertwee.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Troy set out to find a motive for a terminally ill property developer's murder. Many of the Badger's Drift residents have reason to hold a grudge against their charmless neighbour, including a theatre director with unhappy childhood memories, whom Cully gets the chance to work with. Mystery, starring John Nettles, Daniel Casey, Richard Briers and Judy Parfitt.(n)

programme.title: Carry On Don't Lose Your Head (1966)
programme.desc: A cunning English nobleman puts his talent for disguise to good use by rescuing aristocrats from execution during the French Revolution. However, the leader of the secret police is determined to unmask the elusive hero spiriting the nobility out from under the guillotine. Comedy spoof of the Scarlet Pimpernel story, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Joan Sims, Jim Dale and Charles Hawtrey.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Spurred into action by the discovery of a woman's body in Midsomer Worthy's Raven's Wood, Barnaby and Troy re-examine three similar unsolved murders in the same village nine years previously, hoping to get a lead before the killer strikes again. As they dig deeper into the clandestine world of the community, they find themselves embroiled in a sinister web of deceit, violence and mystery. Guest starring Phyllis Logan, and featuring Toby Jones' first appearance in a recurring role as pathologist Dan Peterson.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Travellers descend on the sleepy village of Martyr Warren, antagonising local magistrate Hector Bridges, whose subsequent death by shooting brings matters to a head in dramatic fashion. Barnaby and Troy's investigation uncovers the victim has a history with one of the travellers - but revelations that he terrorised his family suggest that the killer may be closer to home.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Members of the Midsomer-in-the-Marsh Ornithological Society fall out when Ralph Ford claims to have spotted a blue-crested hoopoe, meaning he wins the group's year-listing competition. However, their president Patrick Morgan says the rare species could never have been seen so far away from its native Uganda and brands him a cheat. The next day, Barnaby and Jones have a murder case on their hands when Patrick is found dead at the scene of the reported sighting. Guest starring Paul Nicholls and James Dreyfus.(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer Parva hosts the revival of a traditional pagan festival, but tragedy strikes when curate Alex Dakin is burned alive inside a straw effigy of a woman. The body count rises as an apparent victim of spontaneous combustion, a budding photographer and a newly arrived schoolteacher are the next to be roasted alive. Keith Barron guest stars.(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Julia McKenzie returns as the sleuthing spinster in the first of three murder mysteries. While staying at a hotel in the Caribbean, Miss Marple investigates the death of a fellow guest, refusing to believe he died of a heart attack. With the help of a business tycoon she sets about finding the real cause of the man's demise, and is soon drawn into a web of deceit and dark magic. Adapted by Charlie Higson and co-starring Antony Sher, Robert Webb, Montserrat Lombard, Daniel Rigby, Hermione Norris, MyAnna Buring, Warren Brown and Oliver Ford Davies.(n)

programme.desc: Caroline urges Kathy to call the police about the fire. Kim isn't happy with how Dave acts around potential business clients. Susie tells Zoe that she is leaving.(n)

programme.desc: Hippies colonise Lawson's holiday cottages, causing much local dissent and tut-tutting. programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: An old flame of Mildred's turns up at the Roper house, prompting George to blow the cobwebs off his little black book. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce.(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred

programme.title: Man About the House

programme.title: Foyle's War

programme.title: Midsomer Murders

programme.desc: An old flame of Quincy's is suspected of murdering her rich and powerful husband and trying to evade justice by faking her own death.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo discovers that a murdered private eye had acquired secret police files. He spreads the word that the victim is still alive and adopts his identity in a bid to catch the killer.(n)

programme.title: The Casebook of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A governess is accused of shooting dead her millionaire employer's wife on a remote bridge - but Holmes' deductive powers suggest an alternative explanation to the mystery. Period detective drama, guest starring Daniel Massey, with Jeremy Brett and Edward Hardwicke.(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur's plans to rig the Winchester's quiz night go awry when the questionmaster, an escaped convict, surrenders himself to the police officers present after suffering a turn. Comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster.(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle investigate the murder of a prostitute, but their only clues are a pornographic film and a Eastern bloc-backed plot to blackmail a prominent politician tipped to become a future prime minister. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins.(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Two villains rob an art dealer of a valuable Goya painting, which they plan to sell in Amsterdam. Regan deduces the dealer's female assistant is an accomplice, and after leaning on her sets off to track down the duo. Seventies crime drama, starring John Thaw.(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A group of mobsters try to free their jailed boss by discrediting Quincy, whose expert testimony was responsible for putting him behind bars in the first place.(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.sub-title: I Could Kill My Wife's Lawyer
programme.desc: A boat belonging to one of Theo's fellow detectives is destroyed in an arson attack, and he sets out to prove that his wife's sleazy divorce lawyer is responsible.(n)

programme.title: Championship League Snooker Live
programme.desc: Coverage of day four from Marshall Arena in Milton Keynes, featuring the Group Five round-robin matches involving Mark Selby, Liang Wenbo, Joe O'Connor and Lee Walker, and the Group 14 fixtures involving Gary Wilson, Mattthew Stevens, john Astly and Mitchell Mann.(n)

programme.title: The Incredible Hulk (2008)
programme.desc: A bungled experiment causes a scientist to turn into a monster whenever he gets angry. He travels the world seeking a cure for his condition, while a ruthless general is determined to capture him and use the creature as a weapon. However, one of the soldiers under the officer's command undergoes a terrifying transformation of his own. Comic-book adventure, starring Edward Norton, Liv Tyler, Tim Roth and William Hurt.(n)

programme.title: Inside the Steam Train Museum
programme.desc: A look behind the scenes at Bressingham Steam Museum, revealing what it takes to keep the museum running, from restoring full-size steam engines and rebuilding railway tracks to rehabilitating a wooden cockerel from a set of Victorian gallopers. John and his team of volunteers drive a steam roller to a local country show, Dave has trouble starting a vintage bus, while younger volunteers, Alex and Taz enter into the spirit of the day by cooking lunch in a steam engine the traditional 1940s way. Narrated by Ellie Blake.(n)

programme.title: Fare Dodgers: At War with the Law
programme.desc: In east London, Karen and Peter run a sting operation to catch out passengers fraudulently using discounted travel passes, while Dan solves the case of a long-running scam at Southwark station. Amid the hustle and bustle of London's financial district, eagle-eyed inspector Scott is out to ensure that morning commuters are paying their dues.(n)

programme.title: Secrets of Pompeii: Greatest Treasures
programme.desc: Pompeii is unlike anywhere else - an entire Roman city, buried in disaster, but preserved for the rest of time. From its famous villas to tragic human casts, the streets and houses of Pompeii offer a mesmerising window into the life and times of the ancient Romans. Bettany Hughes sets out to choose just 10 treasures which encapsulate the glory of this incredible city.(n)

programme.title: Ancient Mysteries
programme.sub-title: The Mystery of Rome's Sunken City
programme.desc: Documentary examining the 2017 discovery of underwater ruins along the Tunisian coast that were once a vital Roman outpost, known as Neapolis. Evidence suggests that the metropolis was not completely destroyed, and that its inhabitants attempted to rebuild the city, but a geological phenomenon called liquefaction doomed these efforts. As the land under the city turned into quicksand, the harbour was gradually submerged. Now, more than a millennium since it disappeared, this new discovery is restoring Neapolis's reputation as a jewel in the crown of the Roman Empire.(n)

programme.title: Egypt's Secret Tomb: Revealed
programme.desc: The excavation of a gold coffin, found in the Valley of the Kings, close to the burial place of Tutankhamun. The discovery of the grave in 2006 was the first in the area for more than 80 years. The expedition, headed by renowned archaeologist Otto Schaden, is revealed in step-by-step detail, including possible answers to the mysteries unearthed along the way.(n)

programme.title: Access

programme.title: Portillo's Empire Journey
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels to South Africa to explore how the lure of riches led Britain into barbaric wars, dividing the country along racial lines. He visits the crumbling Fort Napier, the British garrison in Natal, where battles were planned and executed against the Zulu people. The white minority devised rules to subdue the native black population, corralling people into squalid townships and offering them menial jobs. Eventual conflict with the descendants of Dutch settlers - Boers - was inevitable. Michael learns how Mohandas Gandhi fought the racial segregation that would later calcify into Apartheid.(n)

programme.title: Britain's Great Cathedrals with Tony Robinson
programme.desc: Liverpool cathedral is the Church of England's newest cathedral, and the largest in Britain. It was designed in the Gothic style by Giles Gilbert Scott, who also designed the red telephone box. It took 74 years to build and was paid for by money donated from the people of Liverpool. National tragedies such as the sinking of the Titanic - many of whose crew came from Liverpool - and the Hillsborough football disaster are remembered within its walls. It is also where Paul McCartney composed and performed his first classical piece of music, the Liverpool Oratorio.(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: New Zealand's Northern Explorer, the longest-running passenger service in the country. The train travels 423 miles along the historic North Island Main Trunk Line on a 12-hour voyage and the journey takes travellers through some of the most astonishing landscapes of the southern hemisphere from volcanoes to coast and from lava fields to unspoilt islands. Historians explain the history of the railway and tell the tale of the terrible tragedy at Tangiwai, when over 150 people were killed by a volcanic mudflow that hit the train. Narrated by Bill Nighy.(n)

programme.title: Portillo's Empire Journey
programme.desc: Michael Portillo explores how British rule led to armed rebellion in the USA, and a loyalty in Canada that lasted until the very end of the Empire. On Long Island, Michael visits The Brewster House, a former tavern where British soldiers met and talked freely during the Revolutionary War, unaware that it was run by George Washington's Culper Spy Ring. He also calls in at Fort Niagara, where he learns how the native peoples of North America saw their tribes and families torn apart as they picked sides, shedding blood on behalf of either the crown or the patriots.(n)

programme.title: Tommy Cooper: In His Own Words
programme.desc: Michael Parkinson, Jimmy Tarbuck and Chris Tarrant reflect on what made Tommy Cooper one of the greats. Friends and colleagues talk about working with the magician, and his daughter Vicky gives her first television interview. Plus, there are contributions from writer Barry Cryer, choreographer Lionel Blair and actress Sheila Steafell, while Debbie McGee explains the truth behind Cooper's seemingly incompetent magic.(n)

programme.title: Princess Margaret: Rebel Without a Crown
programme.desc: A profile of the Queen's younger sister, who was determined to live her life exactly as she wanted, even if that sometimes meant bringing the monarchy into disrepute. As a child she was as a mischievous and rebellious princess who loved being the centre of attention, while when she split up with photographer Anthony Armstrong-Jones she became the first major royal to get divorced so since Henry VIII.(n)

programme.title: Bouncers
programme.desc: Documentary following the mobile security personnel who protect Britain's cities from lawless disorder, stepping into situations that were once the domain of the police. In the first edition, bouncers Marek and Greg jump into action when two men refuse to leave a nightclub. Further complicating matters, the men claim they have been assaulted by other doormen and call the police.(n)

programme.title: Ancient Mysteries
programme.sub-title: The Mystery of Rome's Sunken City
programme.desc: Documentary examining the 2017 discovery of underwater ruins along the Tunisian coast that were once a vital Roman outpost, known as Neapolis. Evidence suggests that the metropolis was not completely destroyed, and that its inhabitants attempted to rebuild the city, but a geological phenomenon called liquefaction doomed these efforts. As the land under the city turned into quicksand, the harbour was gradually submerged. Now, more than a millennium since it disappeared, this new discovery is restoring Neapolis's reputation as a jewel in the crown of the Roman Empire.(n)

programme.title: Nick's Quest
programme.desc: Nick Baker highlights the plight of South American Orinoco crocodiles as he documents a conservation project in Venezuela to save the species from extinction. In addition to heading out on a dangerous night-time hunt to find out how the creatures are surviving in the wild, he tries to coax a three-year-old to return to the open river.(n)

programme.sub-title: Jane McDonald: Cruising Europe's Great Rivers
programme.desc: Highlights of the singer's voyages along the most famous waterways the continent has to offer.(n)

programme.title: Great Paintings of the World with Andrew Marr
programme.sub-title: Mona Lisa by Leonardo da Vinci
programme.desc: The broadcaster and journalist examines the stories behind famous works of art, beginning by focusing on Leonardo da Vinci's Mona Lisa. He pays a visit to the Louvre, where millions of visitors queue for hours for a fleeting glimpse of the iconic work, and traces the portrait's history, uncovering stories of groundbreaking artistic experimentation and gruesome dissections and one of the most audacious art thefts of the 20th century.(n)

programme.title: Queen Mary: How She Saved the Royals
programme.desc: Documentary about Elizabeth II's grandmother, who as Queen Consort was George V's most trusted adviser and became a symbol of national stability, particularly during the crisis that ensued when her eldest son Edward VIII abdicated. The programme examines her background prior to marriage and her continuing influence on the modern-day House of Windsor, with royal experts and historians revealing how Queen Mary's devotion to her duty and her country always came before her personal emotions.(n)

programme.title: The Story of
programme.desc: The life and times of the artist who has enjoyed more number one hits than any other British solo artist in chart history. Heavily influenced by his hero Elvis Presley in his younger years, Cliff also starred in a string of fun-loving cinema classics such as The Young Ones and Summer Holiday. He has always remained true to his deeply held Christian beliefs, and once memorably entertained the crowds at Wimbledon when rain stopped play. Here, friends, colleagues and journalists discuss his fascinating life, illustrated with performance and interview archive from every period of his career.(n)

programme.title: Ride Lonesome (1959)
programme.desc: A bounty hunter captures young killer Billy John, but his real motive is not the cash reward - he has an old score to settle with his prisoner's brother Frank. As the journey to bring the murderer to justice gets under way, he is counting on a showdown with Frank and accepts the help of two outlaw gunmen for added protection, aware they will try to turn Billy John in themselves for the reward and a pardon. Western, with Randolph Scott, Lee Van Cleef, James Best, James Coburn and Pernell Roberts.(n)

programme.title: Blanche Fury (1948)
programme.desc: A penniless governess is invited to stay with a wealthy relative, where she hopes to secure her future and take over the estate by marrying her infatuated cousin. However, her secret romance with a stablehand has tragic consequences for all concerned. Drama, starring Valerie Hobson, Michael Gough, Stewart Granger and Walter Fitzgerald.(n)

programme.title: Anastasia (1956)
programme.desc: In 1920s Paris, exiled Russians claim they have found the daughter of the last Tsar, who was originally believed to have been killed with the rest of her family. She is taken into the care of a General and groomed until she can convince Anastasia's cousin Prince Paul that she is genuinely the tsarina and inherit a fortune. Historical drama, starring an Oscar-winning Ingrid Bergman, and Yul Brynner.(n)

programme.title: Strategic Air Command (1955)

However, his sporting career is put on hold when he's drafted back into the US Air Force and given the opportunity to fly a revolutionary new jet. Drama, starring James Stewart, Frank Lovejoy, Barry Sullivan, Alex Nicol and June Allyson.(n)

programme.title: Paper Towns (2015)

The next day she disappears without trace, but he discovers she has left him a series of clues, which he and his friends follow in the hope of finding her. Romantic mystery based on the novel by John Green, starring Nat Wolff and Cara Delevingne.(n)

programme.title: Central Intelligence (2016)
programme.desc: Action comedy, starring Dwayne Johnson as a one-time geek who suffered constant humiliation at high school but grew up to be a lethal CIA agent. But when he is targeted by his own organisation, he comes home for a high school reunion, where he enlists the help of the former campus tough guy - now a meek accountant - who very quickly wonders what he has got himself into. Co-starring Kevin Hart and Amy Ryan.(n)

programme.title: Professor Marston and the Wonder Women (2017)
programme.desc: The story of psychologist William Moulton Marston, and his relationship with his wife and their mistress who would inspire the creation of Wonder Woman. Biopic, starring Luke Evans, Rebecca Hall and Bella Heathcote.(n)

programme.desc: A masked criminal commits a series of daring heists for his girlfriend, but a police inspector and a criminal rival are soon on his trail. Action crime comedy, starring John Phillip Law, Marisa Mell, Michel Piccoli and Adolfo Celi.(n)

programme.desc: Construction workers battle against Japanese forces while working with the US navy in the South Pacific during the Second World War, while the head of the company and an officer compete for the affections of a local woman. However, when one recklessly causes a tragedy, he seeks redemption by launching an attack on a tank battalion. Drama, starring John Wayne, Susan Hayward and Dennis O'Keefe.(n)

Western melodrama, starring Robert Mitchum, Teresa Wright and Diana Lynn.(n)

As she wrestles with her feelings, Jane's predicament attracts the attention of a reporter fascinated by her story. Romantic comedy, starring Katherine Heigl, James Marsden and Malin Akerman.(n)

programme.desc: The duo embark upon a family adventure with an invisibility belt. Animated sitcom set in the world of a genius inventor and his less-than-genius grandson.(n)

programme.title: Harley Quinn
programme.desc: While trying to find a lair to house her crew, Harley suffers a paralysing identity crisis. With the aid of Dr Psycho's telepathic powers, the crew must enter Harley's mind to free her from her paralysis, leading to a high-stakes escape from the twisted psyche of a bona fide psycho. Animation, with the voices of Kaley Cuoco and Lake Bell.(n)

programme.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer
programme.desc: A beautiful substitute instantly drives all the male Sunnydale students wild with desire - including Xander. None of them realises she is actually a giant praying mantis on the hunt for young males to mate with and feed on. Only Buffy knows the truth - but she has a hard time convincing anyone else.(n)

programme.title: Rick and Morty
programme.desc: Rick helps Morty to win over his classmate Jessica - the girl of his dreams - by giving his grandson a potion that will make her attracted to him at the school dance. However, everything goes awry when the potion combines with the influenza virus and mutates it, leaving Rick in a race against time to find an antidote.(n)

programme.title: Robot Chicken
programme.sub-title: Things Look Bad for the Streepster
programme.desc: Daniel Tiger is taught the toughest lesson of all, thanks to Robot Chicken. The Nerd looks behind the curtain at Nick's Arcade, while Jabberjaw goes on a much-needed vacation - to Amity Island. Animated comedy series, created by Seth Green and Matthew Senreich.(n)

programme.title: Robot Chicken
programme.desc: The Robot Chicken team discovers the emotions of adulthood in Inside Out 2, the pig from Black Mirror tells all to Howard Stern, while the Nerd forces his way through Star Wars: The Force Awakens. Animated comedy series, created by Seth Green and Matthew Senreich.(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon's obsession with gift-giving etiquette annoys his friends as Christmas approaches. Meanwhile, Leonard is thrilled to meet an award-winning scientist, only to be filled with jealousy when his new colleague takes an interest in Penny. With guest star Michael Trucco (Battlestar Galactica, One Tree Hill).(n)

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon and the gang are excited when Barry Kripke challenges them to a robot duel. Meanwhile, Howard is devastated when Penny finally tells him what she thinks of his chauvinism. Guest starring John Ross Bowie.(n)

programme.title: Naked Attraction
programme.desc: Anna Richardson presents the dating show in which two singletons choose their favourite from a line-up of potential dates who reveal themselves one body part at a time - in all their naked glory. First up is 22-year-old gym addict Sophie from Surrey, who is desperate to find a man who boasts a little banter. Next up is Dom, a 25-year-old wedding model from Bournemouth. He wants to find the perfect man to waltz him down the aisle and thinks people that can guarantee to do this are confident, daring and adventurous in their outlook.(n)

programme.title: Rick and Morty
programme.sub-title: Star Mort Rickturn of the Jerri

programme.title: Harley Quinn
